halve_fix.explanation: Use the fixed-point combinator to define a recursive halving function on natural numbers.

STLCExtended_subst.solution:
Fixpoint subst (x : string) (s : tm) (t : tm) : tm :=
  match t with
  | tm_var y => if String.eqb x y then s else t
  | <{\y:T, t1}> => if String.eqb x y then t else <{\y:T, [x:=s]t1}>
  | <{t1 t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{true}> => <{true}>
  | <{false}> => <{false}>
  | <{if t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if ([x:=s]t1) then ([x:=s]t2) else ([x:=s]t3)}>
  | <{0}> => <{0}>
  | <{succ t1}> => <{succ ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{pred t1}> => <{pred ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{t1 * t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) * ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{if0 t1 then t2 else t3}> => <{if0 ([x:=s]t1) then ([x:=s]t2) else ([x:=s]t3)}>
  | <{let y = t1 in t2}> => if String.eqb x y then <{let y = ([x:=s]t1) in t2}>
                            else <{let y = ([x:=s]t1) in ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{(t1, t2)}> => <{(([x:=s]t1), ([x:=s]t2))}>
  | <{t1.fst}> => <{([x:=s]t1).fst}>
  | <{t1.snd}> => <{([x:=s]t1).snd}>
  | <{inl T2 t1}> => <{inl T2 ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{inr T1 t1}> => <{inr T1 ([x:=s]t1)}>
  | <{case t0 of | inl y1 => t1 | inr y2 => t2}> =>
      <{case ([x:=s]t0) of
        | inl y1 => (if String.eqb x y1 then t1 else [x:=s]t1)
        | inr y2 => (if String.eqb x y2 then t2 else [x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{nil T}> => <{nil T}>
  | <{t1 :: t2}> => <{([x:=s]t1) :: ([x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{case t0 of | nil => t1 | y1 :: y2 => t2}> =>
      <{case ([x:=s]t0) of
        | nil => ([x:=s]t1)
        | y1 :: y2 => (if String.eqb x y1 then t2
                      else if String.eqb x y2 then t2
                      else [x:=s]t2)}>
  | <{fix t1}> => <{fix ([x:=s]t1)}>
  end
where "'[' x ':=' s ']' t" := (subst x s t) (in custom stlc).
STLCExtended_subst.explanation: Extend substitution for all new forms: let, pairs, sums, lists, and fix. Handle binding correctly (skip substitution under bound variables).

STLCExtended_step.solution:
(* Add step rules for new forms:
   - Let: let x = v in t2 --> [x:=v]t2 (when v is a value)
   - Pairs: (v1,v2).fst --> v1, (v1,v2).snd --> v2
   - Sums: case (inl T v) of inl x1 => t1 | inr x2 => t2 --> [x1:=v]t1
   - Lists: case nil of nil => t1 | h::t => t2 --> t1
            case (v1::v2) of nil => t1 | h::t => t2 --> [h:=v1][t:=v2]t2
   - Fix: fix (\x:T.t) --> [x:=fix(\x:T.t)]t
   Plus congruence rules for reducing subexpressions. *)
STLCExtended_step.explanation: Define small-step reduction rules for let-bindings, pair projections, sum case analysis, list case analysis, and fix-point unfolding.

STLCExtended_has_type.solution:
(* Add typing rules for new forms:
   - T_Let: Gamma |-- t1 : T1, (x|->T1;Gamma) |-- t2 : T2 => Gamma |-- let x=t1 in t2 : T2
   - T_Pair: Gamma |-- t1 : T1, Gamma |-- t2 : T2 => Gamma |-- (t1,t2) : T1*T2
   - T_Fst: Gamma |-- t : T1*T2 => Gamma |-- t.fst : T1
   - T_Snd: Gamma |-- t : T1*T2 => Gamma |-- t.snd : T2
   - T_Inl: Gamma |-- t : T1 => Gamma |-- inl T2 t : T1+T2
   - T_Inr: Gamma |-- t : T2 => Gamma |-- inr T1 t : T1+T2
   - T_Case_Sum: case analysis on sums
   - T_Nil: Gamma |-- nil T : List T
   - T_Cons: Gamma |-- t1 : T, Gamma |-- t2 : List T => Gamma |-- t1::t2 : List T
   - T_Case_List: case analysis on lists
   - T_Fix: Gamma |-- t : T->T => Gamma |-- fix t : T *)
STLCExtended_has_type.explanation: Add typing rules for let, pairs, projections, sums, lists, and fix. Each follows standard STLC extension patterns.

STLCExtended_examples.solution: (* Optional exercise: verify typing and reduction of example programs using the extended STLC. *)
STLCExtended_examples.explanation: Prove that example programs (factorial, map, equal, etc.) are well-typed and reduce to expected values.

STLCExtended_progress.explanation: Progress for the extended STLC: every well-typed closed term is a value or can step. Extended to handle all new term forms.

STLCExtended_substitution_preserves_typi.solution:
Theorem substitution_preserves_typing : forall Gamma x U t v T,
  (x |-> U ; Gamma) |-- t \in T ->
  empty |-- v \in U ->
  Gamma |-- [x:=v]t \in T.
Proof.
  intros Gamma x U t v T Ht Hv.
  generalize dependent Gamma. generalize dependent T.
  induction t; intros T Gamma Ht;
    inversion Ht; subst; simpl; eauto.
  - (* var *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. rewrite update_eq in *. injection H1 as H1. subst.
      apply weakening_empty. assumption.
    + apply T_Var. rewrite update_neq in H1; assumption.
  - (* abs *) destruct (String.eqb_spec x s).
    + subst. apply T_Abs. rewrite update_shadow in H4. assumption.
    + apply T_Abs. apply IHt. rewrite update_permute; auto.
Qed.
STLCExtended_substitution_preserves_typi.explanation: Substitution preserves typing in the extended STLC. Same structure as basic STLC but with additional cases for new term forms.

STLCExtended_preservation.solution:
Theorem preservation : forall t t' T,
  empty |-- t \in T ->
  t --> t' ->
  empty |-- t' \in T.
Proof with eauto.
  intros t t' T HT HE.
  generalize dependent t'.
  induction HT; intros t' HE; try solve_by_invert;
    inversion HE; subst...
  - (* beta reduction *)
    inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* let *)
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* fst *) inversion HT; subst...
  - (* snd *) inversion HT; subst...
  - (* case inl *) inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* case inr *) inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
  - (* case nil *) assumption.
  - (* case cons *)
    inversion HT1; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption. eassumption.
  - (* fix *)
    inversion HT; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eassumption.
Qed.
STLCExtended_preservation.explanation: Preservation for extended STLC: each reduction rule preserves types. Beta, let, case, and fix all use the substitution lemma.

STLCExtended.subst.solution:
(* Add these cases to the subst Fixpoint (pairs, let, fix): *)

  (* pairs *)
  | <{(t1, t2)}> => <{( [x:=s] t1, [x:=s] t2 )}>
  | <{t1.fst}> => <{ ([x:=s] t1).fst }>
  | <{t1.snd}> => <{ ([x:=s] t1).snd }>
  (* let *)
  | <{let y = t1 in t2}> =>
      <{let y = [x:=s] t1 in
         $(if String.eqb x y then t2 else <{[x:=s] t2}>) }>
  (* fix *)
  | <{fix t1}> => <{fix ([x:=s] t1)}>
  end
where "'[' x ':=' s ']' t" := (subst x s t) (in custom stlc_tm) : stlc_scope.

(* Substeg tests — reflexivity closes each: *)
Example substeg1 :
  <{ [z:=$0] (let w = z in z) }> = <{ let w = $0 in $0 }>.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example substeg2 :
  <{ [z:=$0] (let w = z in w) }> = <{ let w = $0 in w }>.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
Example substeg3 :
  <{ [z:=$0] (let y = succ $0 in z) }> = <{ let y = succ $0 in $0 }>.
Proof. reflexivity. Qed.
STLCExtended.subst.explanation: Verified definitions: pairs substitute through both components; fst/snd recurse; let takes binder-shadow into account; fix recurses.

STLCExtended.step.solution:
(* Add these rules to the step Inductive (pairs, let, fix): *)

  (* pairs *)
  | ST_Pair1 : forall t1 t1' t2,
        t1 --> t1' ->
        <{(t1, t2)}> --> <{(t1', t2)}>
  | ST_Pair2 : forall v1 t2 t2',
        value v1 ->
        t2 --> t2' ->
        <{(v1, t2)}> --> <{(v1, t2')}>
  | ST_Fst1 : forall t1 t1',
        t1 --> t1' ->
        <{t1.fst}> --> <{t1'.fst}>
  | ST_FstPair : forall v1 v2,
        value v1 ->
        value v2 ->
        <{(v1, v2).fst}> --> v1
  | ST_Snd1 : forall t1 t1',
        t1 --> t1' ->
        <{t1.snd}> --> <{t1'.snd}>
  | ST_SndPair : forall v1 v2,
        value v1 ->
        value v2 ->
        <{(v1, v2).snd}> --> v2
  (* let *)
  | ST_Let1 : forall x t1 t1' t2,
        t1 --> t1' ->
        <{let x = t1 in t2}> --> <{let x = t1' in t2}>
  | ST_LetValue : forall x v1 t2,
        value v1 ->
        <{let x = v1 in t2}> --> <{[x:=v1]t2}>
  (* fix *)
  | ST_Fix1 : forall t1 t1',
        t1 --> t1' ->
        <{fix t1}> --> <{fix t1'}>
  | ST_FixAbs : forall x T1 t1,
        <{fix (\x:T1, t1)}> --> <{[x:=fix (\x:T1, t1)] t1}>
STLCExtended.step.explanation: Verified small-step rules for the extended STLC additions: pairs evaluate left-to-right then project; let evaluates the bound value before substituting; fix unrolls by substituting fix (abs) for the bound name.

STLCExtended.has_type.solution:
(* Add these rules to the has_type Inductive (pairs, let, fix): *)

  (* pairs *)
  | T_Pair : forall Gamma t1 t2 T1 T2,
      <{ Gamma |-- t1 \in T1 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- t2 \in T2 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- (t1, t2) \in T1 * T2 }>
  | T_Fst : forall Gamma t T1 T2,
      <{ Gamma |-- t \in T1 * T2 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- t.fst \in T1 }>
  | T_Snd : forall Gamma t T1 T2,
      <{ Gamma |-- t \in T1 * T2 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- t.snd \in T2 }>
  (* let *)
  | T_Let : forall Gamma x t1 t2 T1 T2,
      <{ Gamma |-- t1 \in T1 }> ->
      <{ x |-> T1 ; Gamma |-- t2 \in T2 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- let x = t1 in t2 \in T2 }>
  (* fix *)
  | T_Fix : forall Gamma t1 T1,
      <{ Gamma |-- t1 \in T1 -> T1 }> ->
      <{ Gamma |-- fix t1 \in T1 }>
STLCExtended.has_type.explanation: Verified typing rules: pair has product type; fst/snd project; let threads the binding into Gamma; fix has same type as a T->T abstraction.

STLCExtended.progress.solution:
(* For the progress theorem, add these cases after T_Unit: *)

  (* pairs *)
  - (* T_Pair *)
    destruct IHHt1; subst...
    + destruct IHHt2; subst...
      * right. destruct H0 as [t2' Hstp]. exists <{(t1, t2')}>...
    + right. destruct H as [t1' Hstp]. exists <{(t1', t2)}>...
  - (* T_Fst *)
    right. destruct IHHt; subst...
    + destruct H; try solve_by_invert.
      exists v1...
    + destruct H as [t' Hstp]. exists <{t'.fst}>...
  - (* T_Snd *)
    right. destruct IHHt; subst...
    + destruct H; try solve_by_invert.
      exists v2...
    + destruct H as [t' Hstp]. exists <{t'.snd}>...
  (* let *)
  - (* T_Let *)
    right. destruct IHHt1 as [Hv1 | [t1' Hs1]]; auto.
    + eexists. eapply ST_LetValue. eassumption.
    + eexists. eapply ST_Let1. eassumption.
  (* fix *)
  - (* T_Fix *)
    right. destruct IHHt as [Hv | [t' Hs]]; auto.
    + inversion Hv; subst; try solve_by_invert.
      eexists. eapply ST_FixAbs.
    + eexists. eapply ST_Fix1. eassumption.
Qed.
STLCExtended.progress.explanation: Verified progress for pairs/fst/snd/let/fix. Pair: propagates through both components, with "both values => pair value". Fst/Snd: reduce via pair extraction. Let: reduces by substitution. Fix: by abstraction unrolling.

STLCExtended.substitution_preserves_typi.solution:
(* For substitution_preserves_typing, add after the tm_lcase case: *)

  - (* tm_let *)
    rename s into y.
    eapply T_Let.
    + eapply IHt1. eassumption.
    + destruct (eqb_spec x y); subst.
      * (* x = y *) rewrite update_shadow in H6. eassumption.
      * (* x <> y *) apply IHt2. rewrite update_permute; auto.
Qed.

(* Other new cases (tm_pair, tm_fst, tm_snd, tm_fix) are closed
   automatically by the outer `eauto` since they have no binders
   that could shadow the substitution variable. *)
STLCExtended.substitution_preserves_typi.explanation: Verified: the only non-trivial new case is tm_let, which involves a binder. Handle x=y (shadow) and x<>y (permute) sub-cases. Pair/fst/snd/fix reduce directly under the outer eauto.

STLCExtended.preservation.solution:
(* For the preservation theorem, add these cases after the lcase cases: *)

  (* fst and snd *)
  - (* ST_FstPair *)
    inversion HT. assumption.
  - (* ST_SndPair *)
    inversion HT. assumption.
  (* let *)
  - (* ST_LetValue *)
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eauto.
  (* fix *)
  - (* ST_FixAbs *)
    inversion HT; subst.
    eapply substitution_preserves_typing; eauto.
Qed.
STLCExtended.preservation.explanation: Verified preservation for pairs/let/fix. Pair/fst/snd values: inversion on T_Pair gives each component typing. Let value: direct application of substitution_preserves_typing. Fix: inversion on the T_Fix premise plus substitution_preserves_typing.
